sourceMovie.title: Hostiles
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Hostiles (2017) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and western blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: News of the World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hostiles clicked for you, News of the World (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Little Big Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hostiles clicked for you, Little Big Man (1970) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hostiles?
faq.answer: Try News of the World, Little Big Man, Jeremiah Johnson—each shares DNA with Hostiles while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hostiles?
faq.answer: Hostiles (2017) is best described as Drama, Western, History, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hostiles worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
